assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and an email address they can verify.
The developer is creating a free Zoom Basic account and will be that account's owner.
No Zoom app, OAuth endpoint, product choice, feature choice, or scopes exist yet.

developer_goal: Create a Zoom account and reach the first local-test success for a new Zoom developer app.

first_success.outcome: A configured development app is authorized and added to the developer's own Zoom account for local testing.
first_success.observable_signal: The Local Test flow completes after Add App Now and Allow.

route_selection.surface: Zoom App Marketplace self-service web flow
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete app-creation route only after the app's intended product, management type, feature path, and scopes are fixed.

candidates.name: General App, user-managed
candidates.reason: The required features, URLs, and scopes depend on the intended app behavior.

candidates.name: General App, admin-managed
candidates.reason: The management type changes who can add the app and which features and scopes are available.

candidates.name: Server-to-Server OAuth or SDK-specific app
candidates.reason: These reach different developer outcomes and cannot be compared as one universal Zoom path.

required_path.action: Enter a birth year and continue from Zoom signup.
required_path.observable_result: Zoom displays the email step for an eligible age.

required_path.action: Enter an email address and continue.
required_path.observable_result: Zoom sends a verification code to the email address.

required_path.action: Enter the emailed verification code and select Verify.
required_path.observable_result: Zoom displays the account details step.

required_path.action: Enter the required account details and continue.
Sign in to the Zoom App Marketplace with the account that has developer permissions.
required_path.observable_result: The Marketplace shows the Developer navigation item when the account has permission.

required_path.action: Choose Developer in the Marketplace navigation.
Select General app and choose Create.

required_fields.label: App type
required_fields.notes: The current source record selected General app, but the corpus-wide route policy must resolve whether this is the correct lowest-friction goal.

required_path.observable_result: Zoom creates the development app shell and generates development credentials.

required_path.action: Complete the required Basic Info configuration for the selected General App route.
required_path.observable_result: The app has the management and OAuth values required for its selected development route.

required_path.action: Select and configure the Zoom product and feature path required by the intended app.
required_path.observable_result: The app has one complete feature path.

required_path.action: Add the minimum scopes required by the selected feature path and explain why each scope is needed.
required_path.observable_result: The app requests the minimum permissions needed for its selected route.

required_path.action: Open Local Test and choose Add App Now.
required_path.observable_result: Zoom displays the authorization request for the developer's account.

required_path.action: Review the requested access and choose Allow.
required_path.observable_result: The development app is authorized and added for local testing.

prerequisites.description: The account must be the owner, an admin, or assigned the Zoom for developers role with View and Edit permissions.
A working OAuth redirect endpoint is required for the selected General App route.

external_gates.description: Developer permissions determine whether the Marketplace displays the Developer navigation item.
The chosen app management type changes who can authorize the app and which features and scopes are available.

unavoidable_waits.description: Zoom must deliver the signup verification code by email.

platform_outcomes.description: Zoom creates the Basic account after the verified signup details are submitted.
Zoom generates development credentials when the General App is created.
Zoom authorizes and adds the configured development app after the developer selects Allow.

excluded.item: Reading Key Concepts and build-flow documentation
excluded.reason: Reading is a documented prerequisite but not a product interaction in the measured path.
The current official quickstart describes the name change as optional because Zoom generates a generic name.

excluded.item: Strict Mode URL and Subdomain check
excluded.reason: The official quickstart labels both settings optional.
The official quickstart presents preview separately from adding the app for the developer's own account.

excluded.item: Marketplace submission, agreement confirmation, review, and publishing
excluded.reason: The Marketplace Development Agreement is documented at app submission, after the selected local-test first success.

sources.title: Getting started guide for new users
Submit apps for review
evidence_supported: Marketplace Development Agreement occurs during app submission

uncertainties.question: Which Zoom app goal, product, management type, feature path, and minimum scopes define the selected first-success route?
uncertainties.impact: These choices change required fields, permissions, implementation work, and whether the local-test terminal is reachable.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose one bounded developer goal, then verify its complete current General App or alternate app-type route against official docs and a fresh-account product walkthrough.

uncertainties.question: Does a fresh developer account currently show a required Marketplace terms-acceptance screen before app creation?
uncertainties.impact: The user reports this step, but current public official documentation only places the Marketplace Development Agreement at app submission.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated screenshot or read-only walkthrough from a fresh authenticated Marketplace developer session.
